flavorText: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.
When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Put 1 random {G} Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

flavorText: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.
It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.
Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.
Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.
It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.
If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.
Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.
The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.
The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.
Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches small bug Pokémon at night that are attracted by light.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.
Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.
The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, once during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot.

flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.
Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.
It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.
It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.
It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it’ll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

attacks.text: Take a {G} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched {G} Pokémon.

flavorText: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the backs of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.
It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.

attacks.text: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.
It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

attacks.text: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.
It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.
This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.
Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.text: Discard 1 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It’s one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Take an amount of {R} Energy from your Energy Zone equal to the number of heads and attach it to your Benched {R} Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.
It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.
Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.
It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

attacks.text: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.
When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.
It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.
When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.
For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.
Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.
Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.
Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.
It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.
It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes -10 damage from attacks.

flavorText: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.
It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.
Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.
Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.
If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.
This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.
An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.
Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It’s said that this Pokémon’s beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.
Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.
It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.
It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may do 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.
It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.
It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.
When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched {L} Pokémon.

flavorText: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.
It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.
The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.
While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.
They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.
When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity when it digests food. It uses its five hard teeth to scrape seaweed off surfaces and eat it.
It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.
A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.
Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.
It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.
It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.
It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

attacks.text: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

flavorText: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {P} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It was created by a scientist after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA-engineering experiments.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {P} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.
It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may take 1 {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to the {P} Pokémon in the Active Spot.

flavorText: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.
While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it’s evidence that Woobat live there.
Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while.
They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.
It loves to bathe in the grit of dry, sandy areas. By sand bathing, the Pokémon rids itself of dirt and moisture clinging to its body.
The drier the area Sandslash lives in, the harder and smoother the Pokémon’s spikes will feel when touched.
It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.
It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.
Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.
Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.
It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.
Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.
It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.
As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.

flavorText: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.
This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.
Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.
Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.
It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.
This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.
In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.
When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.
It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.
By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

attacks.text: Put 1 random Nidoran♂ from your deck onto your Bench.

flavorText: Females are more sensitive to smells than males. While foraging, they’ll use their whiskers to check wind direction and stay downwind of predators.
The horn on its head has atrophied. It’s thought that this happens so Nidorina’s children won’t get poked while their mother is feeding them.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.

flavorText: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.
The horn on a male Nidoran’s forehead contains a powerful poison. This is a very cautious Pokémon, always straining its large ears.
With a horn that’s harder than diamond, this Pokémon goes around shattering boulders as it searches for a moon stone.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.
It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.
It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It’s thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.
Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.
Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.
This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

attacks.text: Take 1 {M} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.
It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.
They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 4 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.

flavorText: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.
A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.
The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.
Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.
People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.
Its reckless nature leads it to stand up to others—even large Pokémon—if it has to protect its territory.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!
When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.
It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Draw 1 card.

flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.
The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.
A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.
An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads—even for a second—will get pecked severely.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.
This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Although it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.
When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.
Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.
Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.
Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck.

flavorText: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon back into their deck.

flavorText: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.
It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.
The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.
Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.
Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

trainer.text:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.
Heal 50 damage from 1 of your {G} Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your {W} Pokémon, and flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.
During this turn, attacks used by your Ninetales, Rapidash, or Magmar do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Put your Muk or Weezing in the Active Spot into your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Take a {F} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to Golem or Onix.
Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Move all {L} Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Raichu, Electrode, or Electabuzz in the Active Spot.

flavorText: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.
Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

attacks.text: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.
This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.
When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.
The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.
It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.

flavorText: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.

flavorText: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.
It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.text: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 4 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.

flavorText: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

attacks.text: Draw 1 card.

flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.
Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck.

flavorText: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.
It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.
